title: Working at the Mizpah mine in 1919; Oil venture in Montana with T. P. Bones
description: Working the Mizpah Mine in 1919. Getting hauled out by bobsled for money. An oil venture in Montana with T. P. Bones. How the Mizpah copper stringers were formed. Search for gold brought people to the far west.

title: Copper Chief, the Olson family mine; Drilling for aluminium during World War II
description: More about Copper Chief, the Olson family mine. Cox, the first miner. Hand drilling for aluminium in clay between Troy and Deary for the government during World War II. Copper Chief gets its name. Get-togethers in the mine; different shafts. Good mineral land couldn't be homesteaded. Diamond drilling for cobalt.

title: Osterberg lies to the people back east about Idaho paradise
description: Osterberg's big lies to the people back east about Idaho paradise.

title: Similarities of two brothers; Brothers sold their homestead
description: The two brothers who act exactly the same way, and always agree with each other. Selling their homestead makes them broken-hearted. Their honesty. People wonder at them.

title: Stories about "Whiskers' Johnson during the depression
description: "Whiskers" Johnson frightens a salesman away. He can't understand why a man looks for work in the Depression. Other stories about him.

title: Stories about Oscar Burkland
description: Oscar Burkland, a wizard with figures. Oscar explains his gift. He gives speeches about germs on Troy's Main Street.

title: Henry Anderson's dream about the future of Bear Creek
description: Henry Anderson's dream about the future of Bear Creek.

title: A piece of leather replaces a burnt-out car bearing
description: A piece of leather replaces a burnt-out car bearing.

title: Carl and John Carmen convert a sawmill in 1912
description: Carl and John Carmen convert a sawmill to live-rollers, surprising the other workers, in 1912.

title: Knutson refuses to rap for burning down Hennings' saloon
description: Hennings squeals when Knutson refuses to pay him $500 promised for burning down his saloon.

title: Charlie Tehyer asks Carl for assistance to commit suicide during the Depression
description: Charlie Tehyer asks Carl to drive him over the county line to kill himself, during the Depression.

title: Anecdotes about Alfred Westlund; Carl Olson relates the fall of the Roman Empire to the decline of the United States
description: Alfred Westlund (nicknamed Mox Mox) can't understand why a better job should pay more. He lends Carl Mark Anthony's book; and Carl finds that the Roman Empire's decline is just like the United States.

title: Carl Olson's amazing experience
description: Carl has an amazing experience, feeling great on a beautiful day. It lasts half an hour. If people could feel so good, there'd be no trouble in the world. (More at minute 24.)

title: A strange experience; Throwing a car off the tracks before and train
description: Another strange experience: Carl throws his new car off the railroad tracks before an oncoming train.

title: An example of the environment shaping people
description: The environment shapes people far more than they do themselves. Clarence Darrow as an example.

title: More examples of the force of the environment
description: Examples of the force of environment: a gray mare brings a couple together; a conversation causes a man to die; what caused Sam to visit Carl.

title: Stories about self-taught genius Brother Bob
description: Brother Bob, a self-taught, retiring genius. He learned how to assay at the University, and prospected widely. He told Carl much of what he remembers of the old times. With no prior experience, he can handle machinery; he saves a steam engine from being destroyed in a smut explosion.

title: Field fires started by smut explosions; IWWs tried to improve conditions
description: IWW's were not responsible for field fires. They were caused by smut explosions, set off by engine sparks. Factors causing smut danger. IWW's attacked because they wanted to improve conditions.

title: Modern problems; Labor strikes; Inflation; Conservative vs. liberal; Wealth concentration
description: Modern problems. Now labor strikes cause inflation; maybe we should work 30 hour week. False distinction between conservative and liberal: a good man is both - he wants to save and to progress. Bad people are misers or wasters. Bad to have concentration of wealth and land ownership. Each worker is as important to the country as every other person. Industry must be controlled.

title: Energy dependence; Country should be run for the people
description: Because we are so dependent on energy, we're the most vulnerable country to war, China the least. Bad planning the country should be run for the people. The stock market a shuck.

title: Labor problems and seasonal work after the Great Depression in 1931
description: Depression didn't hit here until 1931. No big labor problem: seasonal work, and small farms.

title: Life during the Depression; Food and fuel prices; Saving money
description: Depression. Fuel and food were cheap here: The population increased around Troy. People joke about the market crash. Carl does well at his service station; saving money in his safe, he is nicknamed the Second Bank of Troy. Carl deals with poor men that need gas. Effects not bad here.

title: Best and worst economic times
description: Best economic times 1916; worst, the 1893 depression. Sacking bad wheat for sale inside a stove pipe.

title: Isolation and lack of news in the old days
description: Isolation and lack of news in the old days.

title: Becoming Justice-of-the-Peace and road commissioner; The early roads
description: Carl became justice-of-the-peace with two votes, and serves as road commissioner. The early roads; hauling in winter. Cars were put up for the winter; measuring mud holes with sticks.

title: Serving on the Troy City council; Drilling a new well for Troy water supply
description: Carl served on Troy City Council for many years. Council accomplishments for the town. The new well for the Troy water supply (finished in 1973) was Carl's idea, and concerned him for many years. He told them where to drill, based on brother Bob's knowledge. Understanding geologic formations in order to find water. The well a big success.
